forecast-period.text: A high-pressure system south of the state will move east today allowing a strong cold front to move over South Australia later Thursday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate as a stronger high-pressure system moves in from the west from Friday to be south of the state on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning north to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.
West to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots becoming westerly 20 to 30 knots in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 4 metres during the evening.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly during the afternoon and evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.
North to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots tending west to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the early morning then becoming westerly 20 to 30 knots in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots tending southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres offshore.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Northeasterly below 10 knots tending northerly 10 to 15 knots in the early morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Northwesterly 15 to 25 knots increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the middle of the day then tending westerly 15 to 25 knots in the late afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres, tending westerly 2.5 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly during the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers, with possible small hail in the evening.
